content.full_text:
Reed & Davidson
ATTORNEYS AT LAW

DANA W. REED
CARY DAVIDSON
DARRYL R. WOLD
OF COUNSEL

886 WEST SIXTH STREET, 12TH FLOOR
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90017
TELEPHONE (213) 362-9236
FACSIMILE (213) 623-1692

ORANGE COUNTY OFFICE
3151 AIRWAY AVENUE, SUITE M-1
COSTA MESA, CALIFORNIA 92626
TELEPHONE (714) 641-688
FACSIMILE (714) 546-003

January 29, 1990


Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
Division Chief, Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Ms. Pritchard:

This letter requests informal assistance under Government Code, Section 83114(b). I am requesting this advice as a person with a duty to advise other persons relating to their duties under the Political Reform Act ("the Act"). (2 California Code of Regulations, Section 18329(c)(1)).

As treasurer of a recipient committee, we send the enclosed form with an explanatory letter to likely nonmonetary contributors asking such potential contributors to notify us and to complete the form if they should make any in-kind contribution. 1) Does that comply with section 84300(d) of the Government Code, requiring the value of all in-kind contributions of $100 or more to be reported in writing to the recipient upon the request of the recipient? 2) If the maker of a nonmonetary contribution never notifies us, as treasurer, even though we have requested the information, what is our obligation to obtain the information and how should we extract it from contributors?

If you have any questions concerning this request, please feel free to contact us.

Sincerely,

Cary Davidson

CD:lb

Enclosure

NONMONETARY CONTRIBUTION QUESTIONNAIRE


If you made a nonmonetary contribution to _________________________
________________________, please fill out this questionnaire and return it to
the address below.

Name of Contributor ________________________________________
                                (please print)

Address ____________________________________________________

City, State, Zip __________________________________________

Occupation ________________________________________________

Employer (If Self-Employed, Name of Business)_____________

________________________________________________________________

________________________________________________________________

Date of Contribution _______________________________________

Fair Market Value
of Contribution Received____________________________________
                                (estimate if not exactly known)


Short Description of Nonmonetary Contribution

________________________________________________________________

________________________________________________________________

________________________________________________________________

Please mail to:

     Reed & Davidson
     888 West Sixth Street, 12th Floor
     Los Angeles, CA  90017

7/89

sections.question: 1) Does that comply with section 84300 (d) of the Government Code, requiring the value of all in-kind contributions of $100 or more to be reported in writing to the recipient upon the request of the recipient? 2) If the maker of a nonmonetary contribution never notifies us, as treasurer, even though we have requested the information, what is our obligation to obtain the information and how should we extract it from contributors?
sections.question_synthetic: Does a recipient committee's practice of sending a nonmonetary contribution questionnaire to potential contributors comply with Government Code Section 84300(d)? What are the treasurer's obligations to obtain in-kind contribution information if contributors fail to notify the committee despite requests?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a law firm (Reed & Davidson) to the FPPC requesting informal advice. No response or conclusion section is present. The document includes a nonmonetary contribution questionnaire as an enclosure. Document is dated January 29, 1990 and cites Government Code Section 831-14(b) as the authority for requesting informal assistance.

embedding.summary: A law firm representing a recipient committee's treasurer requests informal FPPC advice regarding compliance with in-kind contribution reporting requirements under Government Code Section 84300(d), specifically regarding the use of a nonmonetary contribution questionnaire and obligations when contributors do not respond.
